summary: Investigator produced a well-cited audit of a genuine API-consistency defect (scale=1.0 hardcoded in all inference paths), but the absence of a post-fix metric delta leaves the claim at the level of a static-correctness finding rather than a reproduced bug.

payload.summary: Investigator produced a well-cited audit of a genuine API-consistency defect (scale=1.0 hardcoded in all inference paths), but the absence of a post-fix metric delta leaves the claim at the level of a static-correctness finding rather than a reproduced bug.

checks.label: hypothesis_coverage
checks.note: Four hypotheses span the plausible failure space (train/infer mismatch, JAX/PyTorch asymmetry, prior-distribution init, short-circuit bypass); covers what one would expect for a diffusion-schedule reproducibility audit.

checks.label: evidence_quality
checks.note: Every Finding cites exact file:line locations (args.py:52-53, sampling.py:16-23, likelihood.py:125+155-163, configuration_gidd.py:36, modeling_gidd_hf.py:855-861) tied to concrete Read/Grep calls.

checks.label: fix_minimality
checks.note: No diff was applied, so minimality cannot be verified empirically; the proposed 5-step fix is scoped but item 5 (rename `noise_type` → `hybrid_mixing_shift` with back-compat alias) is scope creep beyond the core plumbing defect.

checks.label: causal_link
checks.note: Per the Dossier's own 'Impact on published results', the identified defect does not affect any a=1 experiment in the paper, so there is no metric that the root cause would move — the `## Metric delta:` section is absent by design, breaking the causal-link requirement.

checks.label: alternative_explanations
checks.note: Remaining-uncertainty section explicitly entertains the 'deliberate simplification vs. bug' alternative and flags the unexamined lm-evaluation-harness fork as a possible confound.

checks.label: uncertainty_honesty
checks.note: Five substantive caveats including the admission that no metric was produced, the uniform-prior init impact wasn't quantified, and the convention-vs-bug judgment is arguable — not padding.

checks.label: suggested_followup
checks.note: Run a small CPU-feasible reproducer that trains ~1M-param GIDD with a=2, a=1, then evaluates likelihood both ways, to show a measurable bpb divergence between training and inference schedules — this converts the static finding into a metric-moving one and would satisfy the contract's `## Metric delta:` requirement.
